Jasminum tomentosum, also known as the hairy jasmine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce symptoms of digestive issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of Jasminum tomentosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Jasminum tomentosum is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory infections.
